title: Capitano Lemnos
summary: Capitano is a wine and deli bar in Kontopouli, Lemnos, opened in June 2025. I designed its two connected digital faces: the one-page brand site and the e-shop.
problem: A new bar on a village square needed one clear address online. The site had to explain what Capitano is, show the space and its food, and route people to either a table booking or the online shop. The shop had a different job: turn the deli's stock into a catalogue that can travel beyond Lemnos, while serving retail customers, businesses, gift buyers, and boats provisioning on the island. I needed both interfaces to feel like parts of the same place.

sections.heading: Context
sections.body: Capitano opened under the chestnut-wood roof of an old building on Kontopouli square. It brings together wines from Lemnos, Greece, and abroad with Italian-focused cured meats and cheeses, signature cocktails, and products to take home. I carried that setting into both designs through a warm dark palette, the serif CAPITANO wordmark, the Wine & Deli subtitle, and a tile and star motif drawn from traditional floor tiles. The landing page speaks for the bar itself. The e-shop extends the same offer into a catalogue that can be ordered beyond the counter.

sections.heading: The landing page
sections.body: I designed capitanolemnos.gr as the front door for a place people may be discovering for the first time. A full-bleed video of the display fridge puts the food, bottles, and atmosphere ahead of explanation. From there, the page answers the practical questions: who Capitano is, what is available, where the catalogues are, what the space looks like, and where to find it in Kontopouli. The navigation keeps booking and the e-shop visible because a visitor may want a table, while someone who has already visited may be looking for a Wine Book or a case to take home.

sections.heading: The e-shop
sections.body: For the e-shop, I designed 18 screens in Figma around the different ways Capitano's stock can leave the bar. A customer can move from wine categories and filters to a product page with images, price, delivery information, and structured details about the bottle. The producers directory gives local Lemnos names a place beside international labels. Wishlist, order tracking, Greek and English, and the 18+ age gate support the retail journey. Separate B2B, gifting, and yachting pages address agreed-price wholesale orders, premium packs with greeting cards, and boats needing the right drinks delivered on time and at the correct temperature.

sections.heading: Output
sections.body: Both designs shipped and are live. Together, they connect Capitano's bar on Kontopouli square with the moment a visitor gets home and wants another bottle or gift box from Lemnos. One site brings people to the table; the other carries a piece of the island beyond it.
